programme.title: Road Open
programme.desc: Djarindjin is a medium-sized Aboriginal community located 170km from Broome in the Kimberley Region of Western Australia, within the shire of Broome.

programme.title: Road Open
programme.desc: Stories from the community in Beagle Bay.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero
programme.sub-title: The Sunrise and Flavours of Horn Island
programme.desc: This episode is a heartfelt tribute to Horn Island's charm, its people, and the deep connection between the community and the sea.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: If you want dinner in 20 minutes, Adam, owner of Bella Bruta Luke Powell, and owner of Jeow Thi Li have got you covered.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Our heroes return to the frozen layer of Icelandis, intent on unlocking the secret of the ghost ship. Embarking alone, Spartakus finally goes to meet the mysterious captain.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Our heroes are surrounded by a troop of Bedouins. Their intrigued chief observes the medallion and then returns it to Zia, apologising for the young thief: a Bedouin never flies.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Aboard the Condor, Esteban is worried about Mendoza, but his friends remind him that their priority is to stop Ambrosius from appropriating Mu wisdom to make weapons.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Little J longs to join 'budda' George on country, while Ally looks up to tidda Big Cuz.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Runaway Child, Running Child And Don't Mess With Bill
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: The Harlem Globetrotters perform for servicemen and their families during a visit to Moody Air Force Base in Georgia. Thunder pays a visit to his hometown of Chesapeake.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: Africa is home to a large number of youth as they constitute 77 percent of the continent's population. A few ambitious youngsters come together to share their vision for the continent's future.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: It's October in Zambia's Luangwa Valley. The hottest and harshest time of year. But this year dry season conditions are exacerbated by a global El Nino event... the Valley is in the throes of drought.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are? Aus
programme.desc: Tom Gleeson unravels the shadowy past of his four times great grandfather Thomas Carpenter, transported to Australia after narrowly escaping the gallows. He also investigates ancestor John Mitton, whose connection to infamous goldfield riots conceals a truth far more complex than family legends suggested.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Living Black returns in 2026 with another series of in depth conversations and investigations. Join Walkley Award winner Karla Grant as she explores the issues facing Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: The Skin of Others
programme.desc: This a story of modern Australia - its violent past and its future potential - told through the extraordinary life of Aboriginal World War I soldier Douglas Grant (1885-1951). Featuring acclaimed Indigenous actor Balang Tom E Lewis in his final performance (as Douglas Grant) and guest appearances from Max Cullen and Archie Roach, this film movingly interweaves the lives of Grant and Lewis: two truly remarkable men.

programme.title: Redacted
programme.desc: A fictional documentary discusses the effects the Iraq war has had on soldiers and local people through interviews with members of an American military unit, the media and local Iraqis.

programme.title: Trans and Pregnant
programme.desc: A thought provoking and inspiring documentary about two men who are on a journey to parenthood. Not by adoption or surrogacy, but by Frankie, a trans man, carrying the baby.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie visits the Great Ocean Road and catches up with a local Gunditjmara/Kirrae Whurrong songman. He meets a passionate foodologist, and spends time with an entrepreneur with a taste for adventure.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Tama, Maia and Jojo show off their dancing skills and Maia teaches Jojo how to spin her poi.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Kedala, day-time for the ngaangk, the sun and kedalak, night-time is when the miyak the moon comes out.

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: There's a new Tui in the hood. The birds decide to investigate before noticing the Humans getting into a territory fight of their own.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Moort sends Jarra to catch a fat goanna for dinner. While he is trying to find that goanna, Jarra goes into a dark and spooky tunnel.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Back in the village, the children happily reunite with Ambrosius. Mendoza reveals to Esteban that his father is alive and waiting for him. Esteban meets with his father, but their reunion is cut short.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kamil challenges Kayne to hug a sawfish, but to find it he must visit a place where darkness is king amidst waters alive with bull sharks and crocodiles.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Nico reads a superhero book and decides to become the Squirrel Man. Fortunately, the funny adventure will make him realise that doing acrobatics in a tree can be very dangerous.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Tired from travelling, Tehrig must stop in a snowy steppe. There our heroes meet Myra and her grandfather, whose village is regularly looted by warrior Mogokhs.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Connie asks 'Why do the seasons change?' and Grandpa spins a tall tale about four rhubarb-loving monsters who live inside the planet and the little girl who teaches them a lesson.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Flies With Eagles decides to decorate the clearing to make it more comfortable and inviting for her and the Chums.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: It's October in Zambia's Luangwa Valley. The hottest and harshest time of year. But this year dry season conditions are exacerbated by a global El Nino event... the Valley is in the throes of drought.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Living Black returns in 2026 with another series of in depth conversations and investigations. Join Walkley Award winner Karla Grant as she explores the issues facing Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: Lalai Dreamtime
programme.desc: The story of Namarali takes the viewer into pre-colonial Australia to tell a story depicting the spiritual tradition of the people.

programme.title: The Skin of Others
programme.desc: This a story of modern Australia - its violent past and its future potential - told through the extraordinary life of Aboriginal World War I soldier Douglas Grant (1885-1951). Featuring acclaimed Indigenous actor Balang Tom E Lewis in his final performance (as Douglas Grant) and guest appearances from Max Cullen and Archie Roach, this film movingly interweaves the lives of Grant and Lewis: two truly remarkable men.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero
programme.desc: In the final episode of Season 1 of Island Echoes, Nornie Bero visits the breathtaking island of Erub to experience a truly unique local pastime.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Two top chefs, Always Add Lemon author Danielle Alvarez, and Lankan Filling Station's O Tama Carey, join Adam to make top-shelf snacks. It'll be tops.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Our heroes are back in Arkadia, discouraged at not having discovered the second Orichalcum.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The children finally manage to penetrate the ziggurat. They start their descent in search of the sacred sails of Princess Rana'Ori in the bowels of the pyramid, where they should be found.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Zares and Laguerra capture the children and push them towards the exit, revealing that he possesses another way towards the fourth city of gold. They just need them and their medallions to open it.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Nanna's away for a few days and Little J is worried. Why does everything have to change?

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Stop in the Name of Love/I Wish
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Cheese and Wham learn about kids on the spectrum at \.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: Indian Country Today News
programme.desc: An independent news channel that serves Indigenous communities with news, entertainment, and opinion.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: There's been no rain for half a year. Lagoons have dried to hard, cracked mud. The scars of waterless streams and creeks crisscross the land.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.desc: As darkness falls, Quatsino's Coast Guard Auxiliary crew conducts a shoreline search for an overdue paddleboarder.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.sub-title: Fires From The Earth The Tseax Cone
programme.desc: In a bay rippling with submerged logs, Nisga'a's Coastal Nations Coast Guard Auxiliary responds to a vessel in distress.

programme.title: The Long Kiss Goodnight
programme.desc: Samantha Caine suffers from amnesia. Her mysterious past begins to haunt her, which sets her off on a search to discover her true identity.

programme.title: After Sherman
programme.desc: Beautifully layered and expressionistic, this is a story about inheritance and the tension that defines our collective American history, especially Black history.

programme.title: Milpirri - Winds of Change
programme.desc: It's early October in 2012, and Steve Jampijinpa is leaving Canberra for his home in outback Lajamanu. Once there, he'll play a key part in something very important to him: Milipirri, a biennial community festival of dance, art and ceremony. But things are changing for Steve and his community. This documentary reveals what goes on behind the scenes of a controversial and ground-breaking Walpiri event.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie is in Waiben, also known as Thursday Island, and meets a down-to-earth landscaper, an oyster farmer who's found his niche in the world, and a Kaurereg Elder holding onto culture.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: NITV On The Road
programme.sub-title: Nitv On The Road: Laura Festival
programme.desc: This program showcases performances by the traditional dance groups who were at the Laura Aboriginal Dance Festival 2013, with the festival co-ordinator, Raymond Blanco, giving insight into the event.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Series 5 kicks off with one of the most iconic places on the planet: Uluru Kata Tjuta National Park. A two-part episode showcases the breathtaking and ancient landscape.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Series 5 kicks off with one of the most iconic places on the planet: Uluru Kata Tjuta National Park. A two-part episode showcases the breathtaking and ancient landscape.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Meet Tama, Maia, Jojo, Buzz and their whanau Aunty Anika, Wolfy, Oma and Mohio as they learn about numbers.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Kwort Kwobikin, to celebrate is deadly! Moort madja, family get-togethers are deadly!

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: The birds notice a billboard for a chicken restaurant. Have chickens launched their own branded eatery? When they discover the grim truth they set their minds to vengeance.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: When moort is hungry for shellfish called pipis, the family make a trip to the coast to collect some.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: After leaving China under the threat of Zares, our heroes arrive in Japan. They must find the third City of Gold which the infamous Zares promised to release to Athanaos, the father of Esteban.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Find out why Kamil challenges Kayne to wash his hair with camel urine in an episode with the grossest mission yet!

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Runaway Child, Running Wild/Don't Mess With Bill
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Julie walks away from the camp without saying where she is going. The other travellers are worried and Max warns her not to go any further. The canoe adventure takes the travellers to the far north. During this expedition, Julie will understand that getting away from her group can be really dangerous!

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Spartakus and three pirates have been taken prisoner by a Sultana, who reigns over a city where women enslave men. Arkana and Massmedia are forced to form an alliance to try to free their friends.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Stanley asks 'Why do we have names?' and Grandpa tells of a time when everyone in the world was called George and the flying Bear who changed everything!

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Elder Kohkum is always busy. The chums decide to help her get her work done.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: There's been no rain for half a year. Lagoons have dried to hard, cracked mud. The scars of waterless streams and creeks crisscross the land.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.desc: As darkness falls, Quatsino's Coast Guard Auxiliary crew conducts a shoreline search for an overdue paddleboarder.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.sub-title: Fires From The Earth The Tseax Cone
programme.desc: In a bay rippling with submerged logs, Nisga'a's Coastal Nations Coast Guard Auxiliary responds to a vessel in distress.

programme.title: Born to Dance
programme.desc: A coming-of-age tale told through the eyes of Tu, an ambitious young man from Auckland who will do whatever it takes to realise his dream of becoming a professional hip-hop dancer.

programme.title: Road Open
programme.desc: Stories from the community in Broome.

programme.title: Stompem Ground
programme.desc: Singer/Songwriter, Pearl Smith is a Noongar/Yawuru woman from Broome WA. Daughter of legendary music singer and song writer Pudding Smith.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.desc: Derek begins his journey with a blessing for his future travels from local Wardan women, something very important to Indigenous peoples across Australia.
